generatedSentence: Detective Kael, an introverted cybersecurity expert, embraced a myopic lifestyle, a genuine nocturn lurker with a tiny backpack. He was a familiar figure, a whistler in the digital dark, whose only kindling for thought came from solving brittle bottlenecks in gargantuan systems. His orientation, a blend of cowboy and punk geekery, involved an anastigmat lens to visualize data cubes. He often felt like the founder of a lonely Google search, seeing multiple threads. But this case felt different, a twofold challenge with an unrealistic lifestyle at its core, involving dirty data and multiple vermin. A major attack on the tramway system, caused by a cheater's sophisticated attack, had borked the city's infrastructure, creating frenetic energy. Kael could peek into the vulnerability, a dangerous pit of fractures, observing teenagers who seemed to piggybacking on the chaos, lounging in a seemingly washed-out daycare. It felt vulgar, like a joker's plot, leaving citizens encaged in an inflation of fear. The bottleneck was a transformer core, now just a casket of energy, that the cheater had turned into a veritable homerun for disruption. Kael knew it was a crucial moment, an intensity that would determine if he would be victor or be killed. He had to dredge for clues, exhume the data, invoke the ghost of a prior system's grandmothers, whose whimsical nomenclature of kanji codes once ruled. He felt the overload, his mind a knot, as he imagined dinosaurs roaming the rainforest, a fitting metaphor for the outdated tech. A specific oboe tune, an imaginary lullaby for the transformer, was his only hope. In the final push, navigating the Archipelago of the city on a rickshaw, Kael found the source of the attack: a slick, overrated 'medicine' designed to provide 'orthopedics' for data, which had gone horrifically wrong. The lurker, minced to perfection by the system, was now an unavailable memory. His victory was bittersweet; the system was no longer borked, the breakers were reset, but the experience left him with a genuine dread. His task was done, his backpack lighter, and he resolved to never again encounter such vicious technological whim. Nope, not again.

He was a familiar figure, a whistler in the digital dark, whose only kindling for thought came from solving brittle bottlenecks in gargantuan systems. His orientation, a blend of cowboy and punk geekery, involved an anastigmat lens to visualize data cubes. He often felt like the founder of a lonely Google search, seeing multiple threads.

But this case felt different, a twofold challenge with an unrealistic lifestyle at its core, involving dirty data and multiple vermin. A major attack on the tramway system, caused by a cheater's sophisticated attack, had borked the city's infrastructure, creating frenetic energy. Kael could peek into the vulnerability, a dangerous pit of fractures, observing teenagers who seemed to piggybacking on the chaos, lounging in a seemingly washed-out daycare. It felt vulgar, like a joker's plot, leaving citizens encaged in an inflation of fear.

The bottleneck was a transformer core, now just a casket of energy, that the cheater had turned into a veritable homerun for disruption. Kael knew it was a crucial moment, an intensity that would determine if he would be victor or be killed. He had to dredge for clues, exhume the data, invoke the ghost of a prior system's grandmothers, whose whimsical nomenclature of kanji codes once ruled. He felt the overload, his mind a knot, as he imagined dinosaurs roaming the rainforest, a fitting metaphor for the outdated tech. In the final push, navigating the Archipelago of the city on a rickshaw, Kael found the source of the attack: a slick, overrated 'medicine' designed to provide 'orthopedics' for data, which had gone horrifically wrong. imagePrompt: A professional 4-panel comic book page, 2x2 grid, sequential art, cyberpunk comic art, thick white gutters, clean black borders. Protagonist is a lean detective with glowing blue cybernetic eyes, wearing a trench coat made of woven fiber optics and a utility belt with holographic tools. TOP-LEFT PANEL: A lone figure, Kael, sits hunched over a glowing terminal in a dimly lit, high-rise apartment, neon rain streaking down the window, shown from a wide shot, vibrant neon futurism, bold ink outlines, cinematic lighting. TOP-RIGHT PANEL: Close-up of Kael's cybernetic eye, reflecting a chaotic city tramway system with sparks flying and digital glitches, highlighting the intense focus on his face, vibrant neon futurism, bold ink outlines, cinematic lighting. BOTTOM-LEFT PANEL: Kael stands before a massive, ruined transformer core, sparks arcing around it, with ghostly kanji symbols emanating from the wreckage, shown from a low angle looking up at him, vibrant neon futurism, bold ink outlines, cinematic lighting. BOTTOM-RIGHT PANEL: Kael, weary but resolute, rides in a futuristic rickshaw through a neon-drenched city street, looking back at the receding cityscape, shown from an over-the-shoulder shot, vibrant neon futurism, bold ink outlines, cinematic lighting.

definitions.definition: Violent, destructive and cruel.
Savage and aggressive.
Pertaining to vice; characterised by immorality or depravity.
The winner in a fight or contest.
The letter V in the ICAO spelling alphabet.

A knapsack, sometimes mounted on a light frame, but always supported by straps, worn on a person’s back for the purpose of carrying things, especially when hiking, or on a student's back when carrying books.
A similarly placed item containing a parachute or other life-support equipment.
To hike and camp overnight in backcountry with one's gear carried in a backpack
To engage in low-cost, generally urban, travel with minimal luggage and frugal accommodations
To place or carry (an item or items) in a backpack
A confection of caramelized sugar and nuts.
definitions.example: As a child, my favorite candy was peanut brittle.

definitions.definition: Anything resembling this confection, such as flapjack, a cereal bar, etc.
Inflexible, liable to break or snap easily under stress or pressure.
definitions.example: A diamond is hard but brittle.

definitions.definition: Not physically tough or tenacious; apt to break or crumble when bending.
definitions.example: Shortbread is my favorite cold pastry, yet being so brittle it crumbles easily, and a lot goes to waste.

definitions.definition: Said of rocks and minerals with a conchoidal fracture; capable of being knapped or flaked.
Emotionally fragile, easily offended.
definitions.example: What a brittle personality! A little misunderstanding and he's an emotional wreck.

definitions.definition: Diabetes that is characterized by dramatic swings in blood sugar level.
The narrow portion that forms the pouring spout of a bottle; the neck of a bottle.
In traffic, any narrowing of the road, especially resulting in a delay.
(by extension) The part of a process that is too slow or cumbersome.
definitions.example: It is easy to create entries; processing the paperwork is the bottleneck.

definitions.definition: To slow by causing a bottleneck.
definitions.example: The merge bottlenecked the traffic every morning.

definitions.definition: To form a bottleneck.
definitions.example: The traffic bottlenecked at the merge every morning.

definitions.definition: A four-base hit.
definitions.example: The first baseman hit a home run to lead off the ninth.

definitions.definition: The portion of a journey that ends at home.
definitions.example: I'm on the home run.

definitions.definition: A success; especially, a popular success.
definitions.example: The product was a home run.

definitions.definition: Sexual intercourse.
A traditional unrefined sugar used throughout South and South-East Asia.
A small-scale production plant that processes sugar cane.
A hole in the ground.
definitions.example: The meadow around the town is full of old pits.

definitions.definition: An area at a racetrack used for refueling and repairing the vehicles during a race.
definitions.example: Two drivers have already gone into the pit this early in the race.

definitions.definition: A section of the marching band containing mallet percussion instruments and other large percussion instruments too large to march, such as the tam tam. Also, the area on the sidelines where these instruments are placed.
A mine.

A hole or trench in the ground, excavated according to grid coordinates, so that the provenance of any feature observed and any specimen or artifact revealed may be established by precise measurement.
A trading pit.
The bottom part of something.
definitions.example: I felt pain in the pit of my stomach.

definitions.definition: Armpit.
A luggage hold.
A small surface hole or depression, a fossa.
The indented mark left by a pustule, as in smallpox.
The grave, or underworld.
An enclosed area into which gamecocks, dogs, and other animals are brought to fight, or where dogs are trained to kill rats.

Formerly, that part of a theatre, on the floor of the house, below the level of the stage and behind the orchestra; now, in England, commonly the part behind the stalls; in the United States, the parquet; also, the occupants of such a part of a theatre.
Part of a casino which typically holds tables for blackjack, craps, roulette, and other games.
A pit bull terrier.
definitions.example: I'm taking one of my pits to the vet on Thursday.

definitions.definition: A mosh pit.
definitions.example: Because the museum was closed for renovation, the school decided to bring its fourth-graders to the pit at a Cannibal Corpse gig instead.

definitions.definition: (law enforcement, usually used with "maneuver") A maneuver by which a police officer, by use of a police car, nudges the vehicle of a fleeing suspect enough for the suspect's vehicle to lose control and become disabled so the police officer can catch and apprehend the suspect.
The fissile core of a nuclear weapon, commonly made of plutonium surrounded by high-explosive lenses.
(hospital slang) The emergency department.
To make pits in; to mark with little hollows.
definitions.example: Exposure to acid rain pitted the metal.

definitions.definition: To put (an animal) into a pit for fighting.
To bring (something) into opposition with something else.
definitions.example: Are you ready to pit your wits against one of the world's greatest puzzles?

definitions.definition: To return to the pits during a race for refuelling, tyre changes, repairs etc.
A person used for sex, particularly:
A worthless person, particularly:
Short for punk rock, a genre known for short, loud, energetic songs with electric guitars and strong drums.
Short for punk rocker, a musician known for playing punk rock or a fan of the genre.
The larger nonconformist social movement associated with punk rock and its fans.
To pimp.
To forcibly perform anal sex upon an unwilling partner.
definitions.example: Ricky punked his new cell-mates.

definitions.definition: To prank.
definitions.example: I got expelled when I punked the principal.

definitions.definition: (especially with "out") To give up or concede; to act like a wimp.
definitions.example: Jimmy was going to help me with the prank, but he punked (out) at the last minute.

definitions.definition: (often with "out" or "up") To adapt or embellish in the style of the punk movement.
Worthless, contemptible, particularly
Of or concerning punk rock or its associated subculture.
definitions.example: You look very punk with your t-shirt, piercing, and chains.

definitions.definition: To look slyly, or with the eyes half closed, or through a crevice; to peep.
To be only slightly, partially visible, as if peering out from a hiding place.
To retrieve (a value) from a memory address.
Double; duplicate; multiplied by two.
definitions.example: The wheat produced a twofold harvest.

definitions.definition: Having two parts, especially two different parts.
definitions.example: a twofold nature; a twofold sense; a twofold argument

definitions.definition: In a double degree; doubly.
To make (something) dirty.
To stain or tarnish (somebody) with dishonor.
To debase by distorting the real nature of (something).
To become soiled.
Unclean; covered with or containing unpleasant substances such as dirt or grime.
definitions.example: Despite a walk in the rain, my shoes weren't too dirty.

definitions.definition: That makes one unclean; corrupting, infecting.
definitions.example: Don't put that in your mouth, dear. It's dirty.

definitions.definition: Morally unclean; obscene or indecent, especially sexually.
definitions.example: At the reception, Uncle Nick got drunk and told dirty jokes to the bridesmaids.

definitions.definition: Dishonourable; violating accepted standards or rules.
definitions.example: He might have scored, but it was a dirty trick that won him the penalty.

definitions.definition: Corrupt, illegal, or improper.
definitions.example: I won't accept your dirty money!

definitions.definition: Out of tune.
definitions.example: You need to tune that guitar: the G string sounds dirty.

definitions.definition: Of color, discolored by impurities.
definitions.example: The old flag was a dirty white.

definitions.definition: Containing data needing to be written back to memory or disk.
definitions.example: Occasionally it reads the sector into a dirty buffer, which means it needs to sync the dirty buffer first.

definitions.definition: Carrying illegal drugs among one's possessions or inside of one's bloodstream.
definitions.example: None of y'all get into my car if you're dirty.

definitions.definition: Used as an intensifier, especially in conjunction with "great".
definitions.example: He lives in a dirty great mansion.

definitions.definition: Sleety; gusty; stormy.
definitions.example: dirty weather

definitions.definition: Of an alcoholic beverage, especially a cocktail or mixed drink: served with the juice of olives.
definitions.example: dirty martini

definitions.definition: Of food, indulgent in an unhealthy way.
definitions.example: The waiter served dirty burgers to the customers.

definitions.definition: In a dirty manner.
definitions.example: to play dirty

definitions.definition: The system of writing Japanese using Chinese characters.
Any individual Chinese character as used in the Japanese language.
definitions.example: I know about a thousand kanji.

definitions.definition: To start (a fire) or light (a torch, a match, coals, etc.).

A negative reply, no.

definitions.definition: A short-sighted individual.
Near-sighted; unable to see distant objects unaided

Common, usual; of the typical kind.

A jester.

definitions.example: My heart filled with joy.

definitions.definition: To satisfy or obey (an order, request or requirement).

To trim (a yard) so that the wind blows on the after side of the sails.

The contents of a pie, etc.

Prepared wort added to ale to cleanse it.

Very small.

definitions.definition: A kettle hole, sometimes any pothole.
(collective) A group of raptors riding a thermal, especially when migrating.

A kettledrum.
An instance of kettling; a group of protesters or rioters confined in a limited area.
(of the police) To contain demonstrators in a confined area.
A looping of a piece of string or of any other long, flexible material that cannot be untangled without passing one or both ends of the material through its loops.
definitions.example: Climbers must make sure that all knots are both secure and of types that will not weaken the rope.

definitions.definition: (of hair, etc) A tangled clump.
definitions.example: The nurse was brushing knots from the protesting child's hair.

definitions.definition: A maze-like pattern.
A non-self-intersecting closed curve in (e.g., three-dimensional) space that is an abstraction of a knot (in sense 1 above).
definitions.example: A knot can be defined as a non-self-intersecting broken line whose endpoints coincide: when such a knot is constrained to lie in a plane, then it is simply a polygon.

definitions.definition: A difficult situation.
definitions.example: I got into a knot when I inadvertently insulted a policeman.

definitions.definition: The whorl left in lumber by the base of a branch growing out of the tree's trunk.
definitions.example: When preparing to tell stories at a campfire, I like to set aside a pile of pine logs with lots of knots, since they burn brighter and make dramatic pops and cracks.

definitions.definition: Local swelling in a tissue area, especially skin, often due to injury.

definitions.definition: A protuberant joint in a plant.
Any knob, lump, swelling, or protuberance.
The swelling of the bulbus glandis in members of the dog family, Canidae

definitions.example: the knot of the tale

definitions.definition: A node.
A kind of epaulet; a shoulder knot.
A group of people or things.
A bond of union; a connection; a tie.

A unit of speed, equal to one nautical mile per hour. (From the practice of counting the number of knots in the log-line (as it is paid out) in a standard time. Traditionally spaced at one every 1/120 of a mile.)
definitions.example: Cedric claimed his old yacht could make 12 knots.

definitions.definition: To call to mind (something) for some purpose.

definitions.definition: To conjure up with incantations.
definitions.example: This satanist ritual invokes Beelzebub.

definitions.definition: To bring about as an inevitable consequence.
definitions.example: Blasphemy is taboo as it may invoke divine wrath.

definitions.definition: To cause (a program or subroutine) to execute.
definitions.example: Interactive programs let the users enter choices and invoke the corresponding routines.

definitions.definition: A whole number that can be divided by another number with no remainder.
definitions.example: 14, 21 and 70 are multiples of 7

definitions.definition: Price-earnings ratio.
One of a set of the same thing; a duplicate.
A single individual who has multiple personalities.
One of a set of siblings produced by a multiple birth.
A chain store.
A discovery resulting from the work of many people throughout history, not merely the work of the person who makes the final connection.
More than one (followed by plural).
definitions.example: My Swiss Army knife has multiple blades.

definitions.definition: Having more than one element, part, component, or function, having more than one instance, occurring more than once, usually contrary to expectations (can be followed by a singular).
definitions.example: It was a multiple pregnancy: the woman had triplets.

definitions.definition: A person, usually a woman, who is trained to assist women in childbirth, but who is not a physician.
definitions.example: A hundred years ago, a midwife would bring the baby into the world - going to a hospital to deliver a baby was either impossible or unheard of.

Finely chopped.

Weakened, extenuated.
To esteem too highly; to give greater praise than due.
Given an undue amount of credit for quality or merit in a field; not necessarily related to popularity.
definitions.example: My sister is an overrated singer.

definitions.definition: An excessive load.
The damage done, or the outage caused by such a load.

To load excessively
To provide too much power to a circuit
To create different functions for the same name, to be used in different contexts
To fail due to excessive load
A soprano and melody wind instrument in the modern orchestra and wind ensemble. It is a smaller instrument and generally made of grenadilla wood. It is a member of the double reed family.
One who cheats.
An improvised breaker bar made from a length of pipe and a wrench (spanner), usually used to free screws, bolts, etc. that are difficult to remove with a ratchet or wrench alone.
Any one of various common types of small insects or animals which cause harm and annoyance.
definitions.example: The area was plagued by all sorts of vermin: fleas, lice, mice, and rats to name a few.

definitions.definition: Animals that prey on game, such as foxes or weasels.
Obnoxious, or mean and offensive person or people.
definitions.example: Bring these vermin to the Palace of Justice.

definitions.definition: Not realistic.
A style of living that reflects the attitudes and values of a person or group.

The totality of the likes and dislikes of a particular section of the market, especially when expressed in terms of the products and services that they would buy; a marketing strategy based on the self-image of such a group.
An intersection of piping or wiring; an interconnect.
An intersection of data in two or more database tables.
The act of joining something, such as a network.
The lowest upper bound, an operation between pairs of elements in a lattice, denoted by the symbol ∨.
To connect or combine into one; to put together.
definitions.example: The plumber joined the two ends of the broken pipe.

definitions.definition: To come together; to meet.
definitions.example: Parallel lines never join.

definitions.definition: To come into the company of.
definitions.example: I will join you watching the football game as soon as I have finished my work.

definitions.definition: To become a member of.
definitions.example: Many children join a sports club.

definitions.definition: To produce an intersection of data in two or more database tables.
definitions.example: By joining the Customer table on the Product table, we can show each customer's name alongside the products they have ordered.

definitions.definition: To unite in marriage.
To enjoin upon; to command.
To accept, or engage in, as a contest.
definitions.example: to join encounter, battle, or issue

definitions.definition: A weak, shaking movement.
definitions.example: Give the key a jiggle and see if it opens.

definitions.definition: To shake something gently; to rattle or wiggle.
definitions.example: Jiggle the handle and see if the water stops.

definitions.definition: To shake, rattle, or wiggle.
definitions.example: The jelly jiggled in the bowl for a few moments after it was set down.

definitions.definition: One of two options on a true-or-false test.
definitions.example: The student received a failing grade for circling every true and false on her quiz.

definitions.definition: Untrue, not factual, factually incorrect.
Based on factually incorrect premises.
definitions.example: false legislation, false punishment

definitions.definition: Spurious, artificial.
definitions.example: false teeth

definitions.definition: Of a state in Boolean logic that indicates a negative result.
Uttering falsehood; dishonest or deceitful.
definitions.example: a false witness

definitions.definition: Not faithful or loyal, as to obligations, allegiance, vows, etc.; untrue; treacherous.
definitions.example: a false friend, lover, or subject;  false to promises

definitions.definition: Not well founded; not firm or trustworthy; erroneous.
definitions.example: a false conclusion;  a false construction in grammar

definitions.definition: Not essential or permanent, as parts of a structure which are temporary or supplemental.
Used in the vernacular name of a species (or group of species) together with the name of another species to which it is similar in appearance.
definitions.example: false killer whale (a dolphin)

definitions.definition: Out of tune.
In a dishonest and disloyal way; falsely.
The track on which a tram (streetcar) runs.
The system of cables that supports a cable car.
A person between 13 and 19 years of age; an adolescent.
To clean with water.
definitions.example: Dishwashers wash dishes much more efficiently than most humans.

definitions.definition: To move or erode by the force of water in motion.
definitions.example: Heavy rains wash a road or an embankment.

definitions.definition: To separate valuable material (such as gold) from worthless material by the action of flowing water.
To clean oneself with water.
definitions.example: I wash every morning after getting up.

definitions.definition: To cover with water or any liquid; to wet; to fall on and moisten.
definitions.example: Waves wash the shore.

definitions.definition: To move with a lapping or swashing sound; to lap or splash.
definitions.example: to hear the water washing

definitions.definition: To be eroded or carried away by the action of water.
To be cogent, convincing; to withstand critique.
To bear without injury the operation of being washed.
definitions.example: Some calicoes do not wash.

definitions.definition: To be wasted or worn away by the action of water, as by a running or overflowing stream, or by the dashing of the sea; said of road, a beach, etc.
To cover with a thin or watery coat of colour; to tint lightly and thinly.
To overlay with a thin coat of metal.
definitions.example: steel washed with silver

definitions.definition: To cause dephosphorization of (molten pig iron) by adding substances containing iron oxide, and sometimes manganese oxide.
To pass (a gas or gaseous mixture) through or over a liquid for the purpose of purifying it, especially by removing soluble constituents.
A quaint and fanciful idea; a whim; playfully odd behaviour.
An impulsive, illogical or capricious character.
A whim (capstan or vertical drum).
A jigsaw puzzle piece that has been cut into a recognizable shape, as if on a whim; often the shape is representative of the theme of the image used for the puzzle.
To fill with whimsies or whims; to make fantastic; to craze.
In scientific usage, any of the animals belonging to the clade Dinosauria, especially those that existed during the Triassic, Jurassic and Cretaceous periods and are now extinct.
In non-scientific usage, any non-avian dinosaur.
Any extinct reptile, not necessarily belonging to Dinosauria, that existed between about 230 million and 65 million years ago.
A person or organisation that is very old, has very old-fashioned views, or is not willing to change and adapt.
Anything no longer in common use or practice.
A set of rules used for forming the names or terms in a particular field of arts or sciences.
A set of names or terms.
A name.
A forest in a climate with high annual rainfall and no dry season.
A covering of liquid, particularly oil.
definitions.example: Careful in turn three — there's an oil slick on the road.

definitions.definition: Someone who is clever and untrustworthy.
A tool used to make something smooth or even.
A tire with a smooth surface instead of a tread pattern, often used in auto racing.
definitions.example: You'll go much faster if you put on slicks.

definitions.definition: A helicopter.
A camera-ready image to be used by a printer. The "slick" is photographed to produce a negative image which is then used to burn a positive offset plate or other printing device.
definitions.example: The project was delayed because the slick had not been delivered to the printer.

definitions.definition: A wide paring chisel used in joinery.
To make slick.
definitions.example: The surface had been slicked.

definitions.definition: Slippery or smooth due to a covering of liquid; often used to describe appearances.
definitions.example: His large round head was shaved slick.

definitions.definition: Appearing expensive or sophisticated.
definitions.example: They read all kinds of slick magazines.

definitions.definition: Superficially convincing but actually untrustworthy.
definitions.example: That new sales rep is slick. Be sure to read the fine print before you buy anything.

definitions.definition: (often used sarcastically) Clever, making an apparently hard task easy.
definitions.example: Our new process for extracting needles from haystacks is extremely slick.

definitions.definition: Extraordinarily great or special.
definitions.example: That is one slick bicycle: it has all sorts of features!

definitions.definition: Sleek; smooth
An act, instance of, or state of expansion or increase in size, especially by injection of a gas.
definitions.example: The inflation of the balloon took five hours.

definitions.definition: An increase in the general level of prices or in the cost of living.
A decline in the value of money.
An increase in the quantity of money, leading to a devaluation of existing money.
Undue expansion or increase, as of academic grades.
An extremely rapid expansion of the universe, theorised to have occurred very shortly after the big bang.
A hill or mountain.
Any of seven fleshy prominences in the palm of the hand, taken to represent the influences of various heavenly bodies.
definitions.example: the mount of Jupiter

definitions.definition: A bulwark for offence or defence; a mound.
A bank; a fund.
A green hillock in the base of a shield.
The principal current in a flow, such as a river or flow of air
(usually with the) That which is common; the norm.
definitions.example: His ideas were well outside the mainstream, but he presented them intelligently, and we were impressed if not convinced.

definitions.definition: To popularize, to normalize, to render mainstream.
To become mainstream.
To educate (a disabled student) together with non-disabled students.
definitions.example: Mainstreaming has become more common in recent years, as studies have shown that many mainstreamed students with mild learning disabilities learn better than their non-mainstreamed counterparts.

definitions.definition: Used or accepted broadly rather than by small portions of a population or market.
definitions.example: They often carry stories you won't find in the mainstream media.

definitions.definition: Obsessive interest in or enthusiasm for a subject, typically one of specialist or minority interest.
The determination of the relative position of something or someone.
The relative physical position or direction of something.
The construction of a Christian church to have its aisle in an east-west direction with the altar at the east end.
An inclination, tendency or direction.
The ability to orient.
definitions.example: The homing instinct in pigeons is an example of orientation.

definitions.definition: An adjustment to a new environment.
An introduction to a (new) environment.
The direction of print across the page; landscape or portrait.
The choice of which ordered bases are "positively" oriented and which are "negatively" oriented on a real vector space.
The designation of a parametrised curve as "positively" or "negatively" oriented (or "nonorientable"); the analogous description of a surface or hypersurface.
An only child.
Alone in a category.
definitions.example: He is the only doctor for miles.

definitions.definition: Singularly superior; the best.
Without sibling; without a sibling of the same gender.
definitions.example: He is their only son, in fact, an only child.

definitions.definition: Mere.
Without others or anything further; exclusively.
definitions.example: My heart is hers, and hers only.

definitions.definition: No more than; just.
definitions.example: If there were only one more ticket!

definitions.definition: As recently as.
definitions.example: He left only moments ago.

definitions.definition: Used to express surprise or consternation at an action.
definitions.example: She's only gone and run off with the milkman!

definitions.definition: Introduces a disappointing or surprising outcome that renders futile something previously mentioned. See also only to, only for.
definitions.example: I helped him out only for him to betray me.

definitions.definition: Above all others; particularly.
Under the condition that; but.
definitions.example: You're welcome to borrow my bicycle, only please take care of it.

definitions.definition: But; except.
definitions.example: I would enjoy running, only I have this broken leg.

definitions.definition: An unruly lock or section of hair that sticks straight out from the skull or lies at an angle at odds with the rest of an individual's hair, like a whorl or vortex.
(collective) A group of islands.
(by extension) Something scattered around like an archipelago.
definitions.example: The Gulag Archipelago

definitions.definition: Someone who visits someone else; someone staying as a guest.
Someone who pays a visit to a specific place or event; a sightseer or tourist.
(usually in the plural) Someone, or a team, that is playing away from home.
A person authorized to visit an institution to see that it is being managed properly.
An extraterrestrial being on Earth for any reason.
An object which lands or passes by Earth or its orbit.
A head or overseer of an institution such as a college (in which case, equivalent to the university's chancellor) or cathedral or hospital, who resolves disputes, gives ceremonial speeches, etc.
The object in the visitor pattern that performs an operation on the elements of a structure one by one.
Collectively, the viewers of a television program or other video broadcast
(history) A communist who operated underground and was therefore not available for a political leadership position.
Unavailing; ineffective.
Not available.
definitions.example: A cold, reserved man, he was seen as emotionally unavailable.

definitions.definition: (with "the") Something uncertain.
Not certain; unsure.
Not known for certain; questionable.
definitions.example: Tomorrow's weather is uncertain.

definitions.definition: Not yet determined; undecided.
Variable and subject to change.
Fitful or unsteady.
Unpredictable or capricious.
To attach or append something to another (usually larger) object or event.
definitions.example: The popular host can’t claim credit for the trade, though. The idea wasn’t his. He piggybacked off another successful investor who had a history of picking winners.

definitions.definition: To obtain a wireless internet connection by bringing one's own computer within the range of another's wireless connection without that subscriber's permission or knowledge.
To utilize "last-mile" wiring rented from a larger owner ISP by a smaller ISP.
To carry someone on the back or shoulders.
To transport (a lorry/truck) on a flatbed railway waggon
To enter a secured area at the same time along with someone having authorized access; to tailgate
A final examination; a test or examination given at the end of a term or class; the test that concludes a class.
The last round, game or match in a contest, after which the winner is determined.

A contest that narrows a field of contestants (finalists) to ranked positions, usually in numbered places (1st place/prize, 2nd place/prize, etc.) or a winner and numbered runners-up (1st runner-up, etc.).
The final part of a syllable, the combination of medial and rime in phonetics and phonology.
The tonic or keynote of a Gregorian mode, and hence the final note of any conventional melody played in that mode.
Last; ultimate.
definitions.example: final solution;   the final day of a school term

definitions.definition: Conclusive; decisive.
definitions.example: a final judgment;   the battle of Waterloo brought the contest to a final issue

definitions.definition: Respecting an end or object to be gained; respecting the purpose or ultimate end in view.
(grammar) Expressing purpose; as in the term final clause.
Word-final, occurring at the end of a word.
A bed with a tough plastic mattress filled with water.
(law enforcement) A police officer who looks for evidence as part of solving a crime; an investigator.
A person employed to find information not otherwise available to the public.
Employed in detecting.
A two-wheeled carriage pulled along by a person.
To move someone by means of a rickshaw.
To dig out of the ground; to take out of a place of burial; to disinter.
definitions.example: The archeologist exhumed artifacts from the ground with a shovel.

definitions.definition: To uncover; to bring to light.
To get on a boat or ship or (outside the USA) an aeroplane.
definitions.example: All passengers please embark now.

definitions.definition: To start, begin.
definitions.example: Phil embarked on his journey yesterday.

definitions.definition: To cause to go on board a vessel or boat; to put on shipboard.
To engage, enlist, or invest (as persons, money, etc.) in any affair.
definitions.example: He embarked his fortune in trade.

definitions.definition: Imagination; fancy.
An imaginary quantity.
The set of values, institutions, laws, and symbols common to a particular social group and the corresponding society through which people imagine their social whole.
Existing only in the imagination.
definitions.example: Santa Claus is imaginary.

definitions.definition: (of a number) Having no real part; that part of a complex number which is a multiple of the square root of -1.
A substance which specifically promotes healing when ingested or consumed in some way.
A treatment or cure.
The study of the cause, diagnosis, prognosis and treatment of disease or illness.
definitions.example: She's studying medicine at university because she wants to be a doctor in the future.

definitions.definition: The profession of physicians, surgeons and related specialisms; those who practice medicine.
Ritual magic used, as by a medicine man, to promote a desired outcome in healing, hunting, warfare etc.
Among the Native Americans, any object supposed to give control over natural or magical forces, to act as a protective charm, or to cause healing.
Black magic, superstition.
A philter or love potion.
A physician.
Recreational drugs, especially alcoholic drinks.
To treat with medicine.
The branch of medicine that deals with the prevention or correction of disorders of the bones and associated muscles and joints
Security against electronic attacks such as cyberwarfare.
A skirmish; an encounter.
A fight with stones between two parties of boys.
A wrangle; also, a noise, as in angry contention.
The process by which selective eating clubs at Princeton University choose new members.
To quarrel in a tiresome, insulting manner.
definitions.example: They bickered about dinner every evening.

definitions.definition: To brawl or move tremulously, quiver, shimmer (of a water stream, light, flame, etc.)
(of rain) To patter.
To skirmish; to exchange blows; to fight.
One who lurks.
A small fishing-boat.
To relax; to spend time lazily; to stand, sit, or recline, in an indolent manner.
The act of one who lounges.
An instance of breaking, a place where something has broken.
A break in bone or cartilage.
A fault or crack in a rock.
To break, or cause something to break.
To amuse (a person) greatly; to split someone's sides.
One who is frenetic.
Fast, harried; having extreme enthusiasm or energy.
definitions.example: After a week of working at a frenetic pace, she was ready for Saturday.

definitions.definition: Mentally deranged, insane.
Characterised by manifestations of delirium or madness.

(toys) A toy in the Transformers toyline which has mechanical parts that allow it to be altered in appearance from its original form as a humanoid robot action figure to another form, usually a vehicle, depending on the toy.
One of the characters in the Transformers franchise who is an alien humanoid robot that can mechanically alter its appearance, or "transform", into a vehicle, creature, or (rarely) a tool.
Something that transforms, changing its own or another thing's shape.

A static device that transfers electric energy from one circuit to another by magnetic coupling. Their main use is to transfer energy between different voltage levels, which allows choosing most appropriate voltage for power generation, transmission and distribution separately.
Agent noun of track; one who, or that which, tracks or pursues, as a man or dog that follows game.
In an organ, a light strip of wood connecting (in path) a key and a pallet, to communicate motion by pulling.
A type of computer software for composing music by aligning samples on parallel timelines.
A musician who writes music in a tracker.
A computer program that monitors something.
A tracker mortgage.
Daytime supervision, usually of children.
To put to death; to extinguish the life of.
definitions.example: Smoking kills more people each year than alcohol and drugs combined.

definitions.definition: To render inoperative.
definitions.example: He killed the engine and turned off the headlights, but remained in the car, waiting.

definitions.definition: To stop, cease or render void; to terminate.
definitions.example: My computer wouldn't respond until I killed some of the running processes.

definitions.definition: To amaze, exceed, stun or otherwise incapacitate.
definitions.example: That joke always kills me.

definitions.definition: To cause great pain, discomfort or distress to.
definitions.example: These tight shoes are killing my feet.

definitions.definition: To produce feelings of dissatisfaction or revulsion in.
definitions.example: It kills me to learn how many poor people are practically starving in this country while rich moguls spend such outrageous amounts on useless luxuries.

definitions.definition: To use up or to waste.
definitions.example: He told the bartender, pointing at the bottle of scotch he planned to consume, "Leave it, I'm going to kill the bottle."

definitions.definition: To exert an overwhelming effect on.
definitions.example: Between the two of us, we killed the rest of the case of beer.

definitions.definition: To overpower, overwhelm or defeat.
definitions.example: The team had absolutely killed their traditional rivals, and the local sports bars were raucous with celebrations.

definitions.definition: To force a company out of business.
To produce intense pain.
definitions.example: You don't ever want to get rabies. The doctor will have to give you multiple shots and they really kill.

definitions.definition: To punish severely.
definitions.example: My parents are going to kill me!

definitions.definition: To strike (a ball, etc.) with such force and placement as to make a shot that is impossible to defend against, usually winning a point.
To cause (a ball, etc.) to be out of play, resulting in a stoppage of gameplay.
To succeed with an audience, especially in comedy.
To cause to assume the value zero.
(IRC) To disconnect (a user) involuntarily from the network.
To deadmelt.
(of steel) deoxidized
A dish or foodstuff where two or more slices of bread serve as the wrapper or container of some other food.
(by extension) Any combination formed by layering one type of material between two layers of some other material.
A layer cake or sandwich cake.
To place one item between two other, usually flat, items
To put or set something between two others, in time.
To lock inside a cage; to imprison.
Turned or thrust inward, particularly:
The quality of being intense.
The degree of strength.
Time-averaged energy flux (the ratio of average power to the area through which the power "flows"); irradiance.
Can mean any of radiant intensity, luminous intensity or irradiance.
The severity of an earthquake in terms of its effects on the earth's surface, and buildings. The value depends on the distance from the epicentre, and is not to be confused with the magnitude.
The quality of being radiant, shining, bright or splendid.
The flux of radiation emitted per unit solid angle in a given direction by a unit area of a source.
A military rank between captain and lieutenant colonel.
definitions.example: He used to be a major in the army.

definitions.definition: To deliver googlies.
To move as a ball in a googly.
An attempt to cause damage, injury to, or death of opponent or enemy.
An attempt to detract from the worth or credibility of, a person, position, idea, object, or thing, by physical, verbal, emotional, or other assault.
definitions.example: They claimed the censorship of the article was an attack on free speech.

definitions.definition: A time in which one attacks; the offence of a battle.
definitions.example: The army timed their attack to coincide with the local celebrations.

definitions.definition: (by extension) The beginning of active operations on anything.
definitions.example: Having washed the plates from dinner, I made an attack on the laundry.

definitions.definition: An attempt to exploit a vulnerability in a computer system.
Collectively, the bowlers of a cricket side.
Any contact with the ball other than a serve or block which sends the ball across the plane of the net.
The three attackmen on the field or all the attackmen of a team.
The sudden onset of a disease or condition.
definitions.example: I've had an attack of the flu.

definitions.definition: An active episode of a chronic or recurrent disease.
The onset of a musical note, particularly with respect to the strength (and duration) of that onset.

(audio) The amount of time it takes for the volume of an audio signal to go from zero to maximum level (e.g. an audio waveform representing a snare drum hit would feature a very fast attack, whereas that of a wave washing to shore would feature a slow attack).
To apply violent force to someone or something.
definitions.example: This species of snake will only attack humans if it feels threatened.

definitions.definition: To aggressively challenge a person, idea, etc., with words (particularly in newspaper headlines, because it typesets into less space than "criticize" or similar).
definitions.example: She published an article attacking the recent pay cuts.

definitions.definition: To begin to affect; to act upon injuriously or destructively; to begin to decompose or waste.
To deal with something in a direct way; to set to work upon.
definitions.example: I attacked the meal with a hearty appetite.

definitions.definition: To aim balls at the batsman’s wicket.
To set a field, or bowl in a manner designed to get wickets.
To bat aggressively, so as to score runs quickly.
To move forward in an active attempt to score a point, as opposed to trying not to concede.
To accelerate quickly in an attempt to get ahead of the other riders.
(US politics) To defeat a person's appointment or election, judicial nomination, etc., through a concerted attack on the person's character, background, and philosophy.
To misconfigure, break, or damage, especially a computer or other complex device.
To become broken or damaged, especially of a computer or other complex device.
Particularly of computers or other complex devices: broken, damaged, out of order.
Something that breaks.
A machine for breaking rocks, or for breaking coal at the mines
The building in which such a machine is placed.
A person who specializes in breaking things.
(chiefly in the plural) A wave breaking into foam against the shore, or against a sandbank, or a rock or reef near the surface, considered a useful warning to ships of an underwater hazard
A breakdancer.
A user of CB radio.
A small cask of liquid kept permanently in a ship's boat in case of shipwreck.
One who works in shipbreaking.
